{"key_event":"Progressive regulatory state-building amid Jim Crow and reform mobilization","reasoning":"Federal power rose through imperial governance, antitrust and railroad regulation, food and drug laws, conservation authority, and new investigative capacity, though Lochner-era courts and the 1907 Panic revealed limits. Civil society gained from muckrakers, reform clubs, settlement networks, labor organization, and early civil-rights organizing, but the net rise was small because Jim Crow disfranchisement, racial terror, corporate consolidation, and machine politics sharply constrained participation for many groups.","society_change":0.1,"society_power":7.8,"state_change":0.4,"state_power":8.4}
